varieties.name: Mini Bottle Gourd
varieties.traits: Fast-growing climbing vine, small bottle-shaped fruits up to 15cm long
varieties.flesh_fruit: Green when young for cooking; ripens to orange with thick pumpkin-like skin when dried
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane subtropical warm humid climate. Suitable for Brisbane, Bundaberg, Gold Coast areas. Recommended for craft uses and decoration when mature. Thrives in warm humid summers.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection Australia, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Bottle Gourd Long Type
varieties.traits: Climbing vine, milky green fruits with white flesh, long cylindrical shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Excellent soft vegetable with delicate flavor for sweet and spicy dishes
varieties.yield_notes: Spring to summer crop, good productivity
varieties.growing_notes: Thrives in Brisbane subtropical conditions. Prefers full sun and well-drained soil. Suitable for spring-summer planting in Queensland.

varieties.name: Calabash (Bottle Gourd)
varieties.traits: Fast-growing annual vine, unique bottle-shaped fruits, nutritious and versatile
varieties.flesh_fruit: Young green gourds tender like zucchini; mature fruits become durable decorations

varieties.name: Kobu (Round Bottle Gourd)
varieties.traits: Round shape with milder taste, climbing gourd, white scented flowers
varieties.flesh_fruit: Taste similar to green beans, one of healthiest vegetables
varieties.yield_notes: Harvest 60-75 days when green fruit is young
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for Warwick Queensland and Brisbane regions. Germination 24-30°C, requires trellis. Well-adapted to subtropical warm weather.

varieties.name: Snake Gourd (Trichosanthes cucumerina)
varieties.traits: Long slender fruits up to 150cm, twisting serpentine shape, rapid-growing vine
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft-skinned immature fruit, mild mucilaginous flesh. Green with white stripes when young
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, vitamin and mineral rich
varieties.growing_notes: Grows best in Sydney, Perth and tropics. Well-suited to Brisbane subtropical climate. Thrives in warm weather with water. Can be trained on trellis.

varieties.name: Round Bottle Gourd
varieties.traits: Versatile fast-growing tropical vine, round-shaped fruits, vigorous vines up to 6m
varieties.flesh_fruit: Young gourds tender when 20-30cm. Mild flavor for soups, curries, stir-fries
varieties.growing_notes: Thrives in warm humid subtropical climates like Brisbane. Not frost-tolerant. Prefers long warm growing seasons.

varieties.name: Birdhouse Bottle Gourd
varieties.traits: Rounded bottom with narrow center and bulb at stem end, unique bottle shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Hard-shelled when mature, edible when young. Interior resembles hollow tree when dried
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent heat tolerance for Brisbane summers. Popular for making birdhouses and crafts. Requires at least 100 days above 18°C.
varieties.availability: International seed suppliers suitable for Australia

varieties.name: Calabash Long Gourd
varieties.traits: Long bat-shaped gourd, long vined plant
varieties.flesh_fruit: Edible and delicious when harvested young (14-24 inches). Can be eaten raw, steamed, roasted, stir-fried
varieties.yield_notes: Ready to eat 60-75 days, fully mature 100 days

varieties.name: Large Bottle Gourd
varieties.traits: Large bottle-shaped gourds, 35cm long by 13cm wide
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good yields of large uniform fruits
varieties.growing_notes: Great for Brisbane subtropical climate. Excellent for making birdhouses and decorations. Suitable for warm humid conditions.

varieties.name: Bottle Gourd Standard
varieties.traits: Hard-shelled fruits, edible when young, vigorous vines
varieties.flesh_fruit: Made into water bottles, utensils when mature. Edible as vegetable when young

varieties.name: Arka Bahar
varieties.origin: Pure line selection from IIHR-20 Karnataka, 1980s-1990s
varieties.traits: Vigorous vines, dark green foliage soft leaf surface, medium-long fruits straight without crook neck
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light green shining fruit skin when tender (1kg weight), good quality flesh
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Kerala monsoon conditions. Tolerant to blossom end rot. Well-adapted to tropical climate. Popular in southern Indian states.

varieties.name: Pusa Naveen
varieties.origin: IARI Pusa New Delhi, selection variety
varieties.traits: Smooth cylindrical fruits, light green color, bottle-shaped
varieties.flesh_fruit: Long straight fruits 30-40cm, excellent for packaging
varieties.growing_notes: Well-suited for Kerala tropical climate. Good for both summer and monsoon seasons. Disease resistant. Suitable for long transport.
varieties.availability: Kerala agricultural universities, Indian seed companies

varieties.name: Pusa Summer Prolific Long
varieties.traits: Long cylindrical fruits, vigorous plant growth
varieties.growing_notes: Popular in Kerala for summer season. Adapted to hot humid conditions. Extensively cultivated throughout tropical India.

varieties.name: Pusa Summer Prolific Round
varieties.traits: Round shape, 15-18cm diameter
varieties.flesh_fruit: Round light green fruits, tender flesh
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Kerala tropical monsoon climate. Generally used throughout India including Kerala. Heat and humidity tolerant.
varieties.availability: Indian seed suppliers, Kerala agricultural stores

varieties.name: Pusa Manjari
varieties.origin: F1 hybrid IARI Pusa (PSPR x Sel.11)
varieties.traits: Round shape, light green color, uniform fruits
varieties.flesh_fruit: Round attractive fruits, good shelf life
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent hybrid for Kerala conditions. Developed for public sector. Widely exported from India. Good disease tolerance.
varieties.availability: Kerala seed outlets, Indian agricultural suppliers

varieties.name: Pusa Meghdoot
varieties.origin: F1 hybrid IARI Pusa (PSPL x Sel.2)
varieties.traits: Long light green fruits, vigorous growth
varieties.growing_notes: Well-suited for Kerala tropical monsoon. Developed by public sector for Indian conditions. Widely cultivated and exported.

varieties.name: Mahyco Varad (MGH 4)
varieties.origin: F1 hybrid by Mahyco Jalna Maharashtra
varieties.traits: Cylindrical bright green fruits 40-45cm long, attractive appearance, longer shelf life
varieties.flesh_fruit: 600-750g weight, attractive green color, excellent quality
varieties.yield_notes: 60-65 t/ha, 145-160 tons per acre, first picking 60-65 days
varieties.growing_notes: Best suited for exports. High yielding with longer shelf life. Appropriate for long distance transportation. Excellent for Kerala commercial cultivation.

varieties.name: Syngenta Vinayak
varieties.traits: Vigorous plant, broader dark green leaves, green glossy fruits, uniform fruit shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Cylindrical long 40-42cm length, green glossy appearance
varieties.yield_notes: Good fruit setting and high yield
varieties.growing_notes: Field tolerance to diseases like Downy Mildew and Powdery Mildew. Excellent for Kerala monsoon conditions with disease pressure.

varieties.name: Clause CBH-11
varieties.traits: High vigor, excellent foliage with big leaves, cylindrical shape, light green color
varieties.flesh_fruit: Long 70cm length, 6.5-7.5cm diameter, 0.8-1kg weight, long attractive fruits
varieties.yield_notes: High fruit set variety, high yield, medium maturity
varieties.growing_notes: Packing and transportation friendly. Excellent for Kerala commercial cultivation. Disease resistant.

varieties.name: VNR Ranjha
varieties.origin: F1 hybrid by VNR Seeds Pvt. Ltd.
varieties.traits: Bulb shaped, light green fruits, high vigor, disease resistance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Large smooth attractive fruits, good shelf life
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, uniform growth and fruit setting
varieties.growing_notes: Exhibits resistance to common bottle gourd diseases. Well-suited for Kerala monsoon conditions with disease pressure.

varieties.name: VNR Haruna
varieties.origin: F1 hybrid by VNR Seeds Pvt. Ltd.
varieties.traits: Very early hybrid, early bulker, long smooth parrot green colored cylindrical fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Attractive parrot green color, smooth texture
varieties.yield_notes: High yield potential, fetches good price in market
varieties.growing_notes: Early maturity ideal for Kerala multiple cropping seasons. Attractive color commands premium prices.

varieties.name: CO-1 (Tamil Nadu)
varieties.traits: Long slender fruits, cylindrical without crook neck, pale green with prominent bottleneck

varieties.name: Punjab Komal

varieties.yield_notes: First harvest 70 days, yields medium to large fruits
varieties.growing_notes: Tolerant to Cucumber Mosaic Virus. Suitable for Kerala tropical climate. Good for hot humid conditions.

varieties.name: Punjab Long
varieties.traits: Vigorous profusely branched plants, cylindrical light green shining fruits

varieties.name: Monsoon (Lagenaria siceraria) 🌱

varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh with slow seed maturity, tender fruits weighing 1.5kg
varieties.growing_notes: Specifically named and adapted for monsoon season. Perfect for Kerala tropical monsoon climate. Indigenous characteristics with excellent rain tolerance.
